title: A searcher that prefers a derived index over the source serves stale results silently
rule: A search tool that loads a precomputed derived index (a built jsonl, an embedding store, a cache) instead of the canonical source silently misses every record added or edited since the last build, and the failure looks like a ranking or threshold bug rather than a data bug. Fix: rebuild the index from source before trusting results; point evals and tests at the same index the tool ships, or rebuild first; and treat 'an exact-string query that should rank first returns nothing' as a staleness signal, not a scorer signal.
triggers: an exact-match query returns nothing or the wrong hit but the record exists in the source files
search returns stale results after adding or editing a record
retrieval tool reads a built index file instead of the source of truth
an eval or test reports a recall failure but the document is present on disk
abstention or a low score on a query whose exact trigger string is in the corpus
rebuilt the source data but search behavior did not change
